transcript.clean_text: another sunny beautiful day on the Welter destination Biryani 186 kilometers in total just one categorized climb along the way but with sunshine and a Tailwind surely this would be one for the Breakaway who on Earth would be tempted with the GC men and their helpers barely having a day off potentially waiting for Hava lambre on stage six this is what lay ahead having almost due south before cutting to buriana that category 2 test coming just a little bit too far away from the Finish but with a non-categorized climb as well leading to a downhill in just 15 kilometers to the line maybe maybe we're in for some drama well they tried over the first 17 or 18 kilometers to establish a breakaway but none were acceptable to the fast men's teams they were all marked out and in the end it was Eric fagundes the Uruguayan it went off up the road and so whilst we settled into an amble of a day I think but through some spectacular scenery in the end the categorized climb was too much for Sepulveda to be ignored he went off the front charged into the gap found fagundes out on the road said get onto my back wheel he couldn't stay there and so the maximum mountains points went for the man in the king of the mountains Jersey extending his lead in that competition the downhill run was something of a test we were hitting speeds of around 80 kilometers per hour but ultimately we were heading for flat land and of course a big Sprint for the day favorite of course Caden Groves but an intermediate Sprint was going to be too much of a Temptation for some of the GC men in particular Remco Eva Nepal led the day by six seconds at the start decided to take a few more seconds on the bonuses six going his way Caden Rose just tucked in behind didn't want to waste the extra Watts but said thank you very much for the points 17 he garnered for finishing second on the intermediate could he add to his tally at the end roundabouts were the problem plenty of them as well to squeeze and elongate the pack as we came in and some of them more slippy than others not a nice scenario to be in and Marlon Menton for a lot of Destiny one of the Sprint favorites for the beginning of the day sadly ending on the tarmac he was okay but his challenge was over and so it was an arrow straight finale we were heading for they had a big turn to actually get to it only 650 meters would be straight in the end it was a question of who could light it up and indeed cope after the last turn a right-hander that gave us our first sight of the finish line well around about three kilometers to go Marca Garrett Thomas said to philippo Garner go for it and he was biding his time Caden Groves has tucked in behind his teammate Ghana went for the center of the road and in fact it was all to come down to the throw in the end the green jersey took it by just a third of a wheel Ghana so close and a fast finishing dries van gestel getting very very tight in making up the podium for total Energies job well done in all departments it seems for Caden Groves and Albertson dikernic they've lost a man on that crash in the end they didn't need him super work Caden Groves delivery yet again Dynasty finishing in fourth place again a disappointment slightly out of position and maybe again hampered by the incident Grove's taking a head of God over gestal donazi asking good finishing turns likewise as per yesterday Gonzalez Jeffrey soup it's Kara also in the frame welcome enhances his lead overall with a very big day still to come 11 seconds his Advantage courtesy of the bonus seconds he picked up on the intermediate and this is what we'll be dealing with on stage six it is Hava lambre always Garner's drama and it is an incredible Peak with much of the finale run over 13 gradients it is Gonna Hurt who's gonna come out on top you'll have to tune in to find out thank you
